title: Shattered Reflections in the Conservatory
openingIncident: At 8:55 pm, Eleanor Whitcombe, renowned art dealer, was found dead in a sunlit conservatory table set with a glass vase, a bottle of sherry, a porcelain plate and a kitchen knife. Three people had been present during the final minutes, and each gave a different account. Nothing outside the room is needed to identify the killer.

suspects.name: Gareth Harlow
suspects.statement: At 8:45 pm, Victor Lyle helped me set the glass down on the side table. We left the room together immediately afterwards. Clara Beaumont and Eleanor Whitcombe were still inside.
suspects.alibiClaim: Victor Lyle and I were together outside the room from 8:45 pm until the discovery at 8:55 pm.

suspects.name: Clara Beaumont
suspects.statement: I left at 8:40 pm. I never touched the knife and did not go near the bottle.
suspects.alibiClaim: I had already left the room by 8:40 pm and did not return.

suspects.name: Victor Lyle
suspects.statement: At 8:45 pm, while Gareth Harlow set the glass down on the side table, I set the bottle down on the side table. We then left together and remained together until the body was found at 8:55 pm.
suspects.alibiClaim: Gareth Harlow and I were together outside the room from 8:45 pm until the discovery at 8:55 pm.

clues.title: The Edge of Knife
clues.detail: A fresh line of blood runs along one edge of the knife, and its point has a new bend. Eleanor Whitcombe has a narrow wound consistent with that edge.

clues.title: Ring beneath Glass
clues.detail: The fresh circular ring beneath the glass is crossed by a narrow blood droplet from the attack whose edge remains unbroken. Gareth Harlow and Victor Lyle both state that they set the glass down on the side table at 8:45 pm before leaving together.

clues.title: Fibres on Knife
clues.detail: Fine copper-coloured silk threads are caught around the handle of the knife. Clara Beaumont's copper silk pocket square has a fresh narrow tear, exposing the same alternating copper and navy stripe.

clues.title: Ring beneath Bottle
clues.detail: The fresh circular ring beneath the bottle is crossed by a narrow blood droplet from the attack. Victor Lyle states that they set the bottle down on the side table at 8:45 pm immediately before leaving the room.

clues.title: Crack in Plate
clues.detail: A crack runs across the plate. A continuous yellowed glue line is visible deep inside the break.

solution.motive: Clara Beaumont killed Eleanor Whitcombe because the imminent exposure of his embezzlement scheme.
solution.method: Eleanor Whitcombe was fatally stabbed with the knife.
solution.timeOfDeath: After 8:45 pm and before discovery at 8:55 pm
solution.opportunity: Clara Beaumont claimed to have left at 8:40 pm. The physical trace on the glass corroborates the action described by Gareth Harlow, while the trace on the bottle corroborates the action described by Victor Lyle. They left together at 8:45 pm, and both traces are crossed by evidence from the later attack. Meanwhile, fine copper-coloured silk threads on the knife directly contradict Clara Beaumont's denial and place them at the murder object during the attack window.
